items.title: 10 jumps, 20 shots, 4 rounds.
items.author: M脙楼lvaktsfokus
items.description:
10 jumps, 20 shots, 4 rounds.@lara.heini 冒聼聰楼
-
Physical training does not need to be, and should not be complicated. 

All of these moves are likely to happen in a game.

1) Jump up and try to catch a high ball.
2) Shortly after, an opportunity for a save appears, so you have to quickly regain your basic stance.
3) Player passes instead of shoots, or the rebound goes to the side, so you have to get there quickly for the save.

芒聙垄 Repeat in quick succession for 20 shots. 
芒聙垄 Rest for as long as it took you to do the 20 shots.
芒聙垄 Then go for as many rounds as you need to get your heart rate up (preferably to 90% or higher).

Why? 

Two main reasons.
Interval training is the most effective way to increase your heart and lung capacity, and the exercise forces you to keep focus even if you are exhausted.

Techniques and saves are secondary in this exercise.

Also, because it's fun 冒聼聵聝

Save it and try it!

A discussion from my visit to @uhcbremgarten 冒聼聶聦

Here is what we 芒聹聟茂赂聫 ARE 芒聹聟茂赂聫 talking about in this video:
芒聙垄 A player quickly moving the ball from backhand to forehand and shooting a quick wrist shot on my far post.
芒聙垄 How much or little do I have to move to cover my far post at this specific angle? 
芒聙垄 Do I need to move my knees at all, or can I just tilt my torso?

Here is what we are 冒聼職芦 NOT 冒聼職芦 talking about in this video:
芒聙垄 What about leaving that much space on the first post? 
芒聙垄 What if the player keeps the ball and runs around me?
芒聙垄 Can't you just attack the player or move out of the net? 

These are all valid points of discussion as well, but not a topic in this clip. 

In this imagined situation, I have read the player and I KNOW they will shoot on my far post. 

I have also timed the stick move perfectly, so the first post won't be open until the shot is made on the far post.

There is not enough time to move out, because the situation happened right after a lateral move, so I have no time or momentum to move out/attack the player.

The takeaway from this is simply:
芒聹聟茂赂聫 How wide is the goal from this angle?

items.title: Hand-eye coordination!
items.author: M脙楼lvaktsfokus
items.description:
Hand-eye coordination! 冒聼聰楼 @linneawall 
-
Tracking the ball with your eyes from the release point of the blade all the way into your hand or body is so important! 

It helps you: 

芒聺聲茂赂聫Make more saves in general, because it's easier to stop that which you can actually see

芒聺聲茂赂聫 Make saves with more control. It will seem like the ball slows down when successfully tracked.

芒聺聲茂赂聫Catch more shots. Since the ball will seem like it's slowing down, you will be more likely to catch it.

芒聺聲茂赂聫 Better rebounding. If you don't catch it (which you don't have to), you will see where the rebound goes.

The ceiling is really high with this skill, meaning you can get incredibly good at this. It might take time, but it's worth it!
